rag_rules: Insert AGREE annexes only into the target implementation scope when bindings depend on implementation-level context.
Preserve AADL structure by not modifying component types, subcomponents, or connections during annex insertion.
generic_rules: Scope AGREE clauses to the precise AADL element (type or implementation) that owns the referenced ports or properties.
Do not assume AADL properties are visible as AGREE variables; rely only on explicitly modeled ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for all AGREE annexes.
Implementation-level annexes must reference only subcomponents, connections, or ports available in that scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Priority) directly as AGREE variables without explicit typed declarations.
Avoid inserting AGREE annexes into component types when the requirement depends on implementation-specific details.
